key_insight: TACT is a self-supervised, time-aware transformer that learns longitudinal patient representations from irregular EHRs by combining explicit temporal modeling with contrastive learning and medical taxonomies.
clinical_relevance: By better tracking disease trajectories and patient heterogeneity, TACT could help identify clinically meaningful subgroups and support precision oncology research.
method: Transformer model with explicit temporal encoding for irregular event sequences
Fully self-supervised contrastive learning for patient representation and event embeddings
Sampling-based augmentation using hierarchical diagnosis and medication taxonomies
evidence_signal: On a large real-world dataset, TACT outperformed two time-aware transformer baselines on patient representation and event embedding metrics.
watch_item: Results are from retrospective EHR data, so external validation and careful handling of taxonomy quality, missingness, and site-specific coding differences are important.
paper_title: Time-Aware Contrastive Transformer for Longitudinal Patient Representation Learning
